workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Pulling in context from Treyarch Global's meeting transcript -- need to investigate the decline for the renewal risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 09:06 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 13:14 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 14:30 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-I trap (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-I failure mode (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-I -- Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant
